entry.title: 🚀 秒速で学ぶ SAP AI Core Agent 開発 🚀 - Part 5 RAG 基礎 ②: Retriever Toolの構築
entry.content:
生成 AI は研究室の外へ飛び出し、今やビジネス現場の常識を塗り替えています。SAPは全速力でその波に乗っています。このブログシリーズでは、SAP AI Core の既定モデルを最速で呼び出し、実務で使える AI エージェントへ拡張する“秒速ハンズオン”をお届けします。

お知らせ
英語版はこちらで確認できます。

 

📖 本シリーズで学べること

  • SAP AI Core 上でカスタム AI エージェントを “秒速” で動かす方法
  • LangChain・Google 検索ツール・RAG・Streamlit を使った実装
  • エージェントを REST API 化し、UI を SAPUI5/Fiori に載せ替える手順

学習時間
各章は 10–15 分 で読める＆手を動かせるを予定しています。

 

🗺️ 連載ロードマップ

  • Part 0 プロローグ
  • Part 1 環境構築: SAP AI CoreとAI Launchpad
  • Part 2 LangChain でチャットモデルを構築
  • Part 3 Agentツール: Google 検索を統合
  • Part 4 RAG 基礎 ①: HANA Cloud VectorEngineと埋め込み処理
  • Part 5 RAG 基礎 ②: Retriever Toolの構築 [現在のブログ]
  • Part 6 Streamlit 基礎
  • Part 7 Streamlit でチャットボットの構築
  • Part 8 AI Agent を REST API 化
  • Part 9 SAPUI5 でチャットUI を再構築 前編
  • Part 8 SAPUI5 でチャットUI を再構築 後編

注記
続編は順次公開予定です。

この記事がお役に立ったら、ぜひ Kudos を押していただけると励みになります。 「ここをもっと詳しく知りたい」「別のテーマも取り上げてほしい」など、ご要望があればお気軽にコメントください！

RAG 基礎 ②: Retriever Toolの構築

1｜はじめに

前章では社内ドキュメントを SAP HANA Cloud VectorEngine に格納し、ベクトルによるクエリ（質問文）と社内ドキュメントとの類似度検索を実行するところまで完了しました。本章では、その検索ロジックを AI Agent が呼び出せるツール化（Retriever Tool 化）します。これにより、AI Agent は「社内ベクターストア → Web 検索」の二段ロケットで回答を組み立てられるようになります。

 

2 | 事前準備

  • BTP サブアカウント
  • SAP AI Core インスタンス
  • SAP AI Launchpadのサブスクリプション
  • Python 3.13環境 & pip
  • VSCodeやBASなどのIDE

Trial 環境の注意
Trial の HANA Cloud インスタンスは 毎晩自動停止 されます。日をまたぐ作業の場合は翌日インスタンスを再起動してください。

 

3 | Retriever Tool の作成

Retriever とは「クエリと内容が似ているテキストチャンクを返すオブジェクト」で、LangChain の RAG パイプラインで最初に呼ばれます。前章で作った dbインスタンスには既にベクトルとメタデータが保存されているため、これをそのまま Retriever へ昇格させます。

まず、前章で埋め込み処理&格納したデータを HANA Cloud Explorer 上で確認してみましょう。TEST_TABLE テーブルを開くと、テキスト列とベクトル列が格納されているのが確認できます。

 

LangChain における Retriever は .as_retriever() で簡単に生成できます。前章で作った db インスタンス（ベクターストア）をそのまま再利用しましょう。

# ▶ Notebook Cell 11
retriever = db.as_retriever()

# テストクエリを投げて 1 件だけ確認
retriever.invoke("Tell me about SAP security")[0]


続いて、Retriever を ツール化 します。create_retriever_tool に Retriever 本体・ツール名・説明文を渡すだけです。説明文は LLM が読んで使用可否を判断する重要パラメータなので、目的を明確に書きましょう。説明文には「何を検索できるか」「いつ使うべきか」を含めることをオススメします。

# ▶ Notebook Cell 12
from langchain.tools.retriever import create_retriever_tool
retriever_tool = create_retriever_tool(
    retriever=retriever,
    name="hana_vectorengine",
    description=(
        "Use this tool to search internal SAP documents stored in HANA Cloud Vector Engine when the user asks about company-specific policies, security, or best practices."
    ),
)

 

4 | AI Agent に組み込んで動かす

これで Google Search と HANA VectorEngine の 2 本立てツールセットが揃いました。
initialize_agent() で両方を渡して agent インスタンスを作成します。そして、複雑な質問を AI Agent に投げてみましょう！

# ▶ Notebook Cell 13
from langchain.agents import initialize_agent, AgentType

agent = initialize_agent(
    tools=[google_tool, retriever_tool],
    llm=chat_llm,
    agent=AgentType.OPENAI_FUNCTIONS,
    verbose=True,
)

result = agent.invoke("RISE with SAP のサービス内容と、2025 年時点でのクラウド移行市場シェア（他社比較）をあわせて解説してください。")

 

"verbose=True"と指定すると、AI Agent の『Thought → Action → Observation』の流れがそのまま出力されます。下のスクリーンショットにおける、

Invoking: hana_vectorengine with {'query': 'RISE with SAP サービス内容'}

で、まず HANA Cloud VectorEngine で社内ドキュメント検索を行っていることが分かります。
続く赤枠、

Invoking: google_search with {'query': '2025 クラウド移行市場シェア SAP 他社比較'}

で、Google Search による最新市場データの取得を表しています。
（環境によって実行順やクエリ文字列は多少変わる場合があります。）

 

最後に、エージェントの最終回答を Markdown として表示しましょう。
これで本章のゴール――「検索ツールと HANA Cloud VectorEngine による Retriever を併用する AI Agent」の完成です！！

# ▶ Notebook Cell 14
from IPython.display import Markdown, display

display(Markdown(result["output"]))

 

5｜チャレンジ – AI Agent に メモリ を付けてみよう

今回までに作った AI Agent は会話履歴を保持しません。これでは「さっきの質問を踏まえて答える」「ユーザ名を覚える」といった自然な対話ができないため、LangChain の従来メモリクラス ConversationBufferMemory を使って履歴を保存できるように拡張します。

将来的な移行について
ConversationBufferMemory は LangChain 0.3 系で非推奨となり、今後は LangGraph を用いたメモリ実装が推奨されています。
本シリーズではまず “秒速” で動くことを優先し、今回は従来クラスで実装します。後日 Part 5.5 で LangGraph 版の AI Agent ＋ メモリ実装 をあらためて作成し、移行手順まで詳しく取り上げる予定です。

# ▶ Notebook Cell 15
from langchain.chains.conversation.memory import ConversationBufferMemory
from langchain_core.prompts import MessagesPlaceholder
from langchain.agents import initialize_agent, AgentType

agent_kwargs = {
    "extra_prompt_messages": [MessagesPlaceholder(variable_name="memory")],
}

memory = ConversationBufferMemory(memory_key="memory", return_messages=True)

agent_with_memory = initialize_agent(
    tools=[google_tool, retriever_tool],
    llm=chat_llm,
    agent=AgentType.OPENAI_FUNCTIONS,
    verbose=True,
    agent_kwargs=agent_kwargs,
    memory=memory,
)

上のセルでは ①履歴を保持する ConversationBufferMemory を生成し、②initialize_agent に memory 引数として渡しています。MessagesPlaceholder を追加したことで、履歴がプロンプトに自動で差し込まれる仕組みになりました。

続いて動きを確認します。

result1 = agent_with_memory.invoke("私の名前は伊藤です。")
result2 = agent_with_memory.invoke("私の名前は？")
print(result2["output"])

2 回目の呼び出しで「あなたの名前は伊藤さんですね」など前の発話を反映した答えが返ってくれば、メモリが機能しています。出力から履歴が連鎖している様子を確認してみてください。 

メモリ付きになったことで、同じセッション内でのフォローアップ質問や、HANA Cloudでの検索結果・Google 検索結果を踏まえた追加質問が自然に行えるようになります。ぜひ色々試してみてください！

 

6｜次回予告

Part 6 Streamlit 基礎

Part 6 では、まず Streamlit そのものを “秒速” で体験します。チャット欄・送信ボタン・メッセージ履歴といった UI の土台だけ を作り、「ローカルで Web アプリが立ち上がる」ことを確認するところまでを扱います。お楽しみに！

 

免責事項

免責事項 – 本ブログに記載された見解および意見はすべて私個人のものであり、私の個人的な立場で発信しています。SAP は本ブログの内容について一切の責任を負いません。

entry.title: Simplifying Test Management in SAP Cloud ALM: Introducing Test Case Sequences
entry.content:
When managing complex testing processes in SAP Cloud ALM, it's often not enough to simply list test cases alphabetically. End-to-end testing demands a logical sequence—one that mirrors the actual business flow and enables smoother test execution. This is where the new Test Case Sequence feature comes into play.

 

Why Test Case Sequence Matters

Until recently, SAP Cloud ALM displayed test cases alphabetically, which worked well for basic scenarios. However, many testing projects require a predefined order of execution, particularly when there are logical or business process dependencies between tests. Now, with the Add Sequence feature, test managers can define the execution order without the complexity of hardcoded dependencies.

This structured sequence provides:

  • A clear execution path for testers.

  • Better alignment with end-to-end process flows.

  • Improved planning and monitoring during test cycles.

 

Key Capabilities

With the Test Case Sequence feature, users can:

  • Add a Sequence: Assign numbers to test cases to determine their order.

  • Move Test Cases: Reorder test cases easily using numbers in the Sequence column.

  • Maintain Consistency: The defined order in the Test Plan is replicated in the Test Execution app.

  • Error Checking: SAP Cloud ALM ensures no test plan with activated sequence does contain test case without a sequence number, providing validation on save.

  • Copy Test Plans with Sequence: Sequences are retained when copying existing test plans.

 

How It Works

  1. Open the Test Plan
    Navigate to the test plan you’re working on.

  2. Click "Add Sequence"
    The app enables sequence editing even when the test plan is initially empty.

  3. Assign Sequence Numbers
    Use whole numbers (no decimals) to define the execution order. This preferred approach avoids UI complexities.

     

  4. Maintain Order Logic

    • Numbers must be unique.

    • Moving a test case up or down auto-adjusts surrounding test cases.

    • For example, moving test case 5 to position 3 pushes cases 3 and 4 to positions 4 and 5.

 

Where You’ll See It

The sequence becomes especially valuable in the Test Execution app, where testers need clarity on the execution flow. The cases are sorted based on the defined sequence, guiding testers through the planned process efficiently.

 

What This Means for You

Whether you’re preparing for a financial close, rolling out a sales release, or simply organizing regression tests, having the ability to define a logical sequence of execution makes your test plans more actionable and your teams more aligned.

This feature enhances:

  • Traceability – You can now ensure that the sequence aligns with your process documentation.

  • Productivity – Testers follow a clear, predefined path without ambiguity.

  • Governance – Managers can better control and monitor the testing lifecycle.

 

Final Thoughts

The Test Case Sequence feature in SAP Cloud ALM is a small but powerful enhancement that bridges the gap between test planning and execution. It’s a great example of how thoughtful UI and functional updates can simplify test management and drive business value.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud Pong Game - A Fun Fusion of Custom Widgets and Standard Functionalities
entry.content:
Introduction

Have you ever wondered what happens when the worlds of gaming and business analytics collide? Well, today, I am about to take you on an exciting journey where we’ll explore just that. In this blog, I’ll demonstrate a Pong game in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) which combines the power of custom widgets and standard functionalities.

Are you ready to spice up your analytics environment? Let’s dive into it!

 

Why Build a Pong Game in SAP Analytics Cloud?

You might be wondering, 'Why a Pong Game?'. Pong is a classic game, known for its simple mechanics and fun appeal. Furthermore, think about the broad insights you can gain by combining it with a powerful analytics platform like SAP Analytics Cloud. In the end, we want to embed the Pong Game into a SAC story, allowing users to play the game and then review simple statistics. This dynamic interaction should showcase the wide-ranging possibilities with custom widgets and create an engaging experience that might be perfect for some meetings, team-building activities, sales demos, or just making your analytics environment more interactive and fun.

Within this project, we can explore:

  • Custom Widgets:
    Extends SAP Analytics Cloud beyond its standard capabilities by embedding custom visualizations and interactive elements.

  • Planning/Reporting:
    Use SAP’s built-in functionalities to store and track your gaming stats

 

Architecture

 

Building the Pong Game

To play the Pong game within SAP Analytics Cloud, you’ll need to develop a custom widget using at least two components:

  1. JavaScript File for Game Logic (Main.js):
    Write a script to control the movement of the ball and the paddles, handle collision detection and much more. This code will define the game's behavior and interactivity.



  2. JSON File (Index.json):
    This file specifies the custom widget, defining details such as its name, description, and version. It also allows you to define properties, methods, and events, which store game-related data at runtime like player scores or ball speed. You can reference this JSON file in your JavaScript to adjust game settings dynamically based on user interaction.

With these components, you'll be able to create the core functionality of the game. I used Visual Studio Code as development environment but feel free to use a code editor of your choice. In the end you will just upload the mentioned files in SAC, where they’ll be hosted.

 

Storing Custom Widget Game Results in SAC

Unfortunately, there is currently no possibility to directly write data with custom widgets into SAC models. Although there are open APIs like the PUSH based Data Import Service which allows you to import fact and master data to SAP Analytics Cloud, I chose a simpler approach to avoid unnecessary complexity and efforts required to set up such integrations.

Let’s combine the custom widget with SAC’s standard functionalities.

Prerequisites

  1. Custom Widget Properties: 
    Define properties in the custom widget to hold and update the data dynamically, such as the scores for Player 1 and Player 2. I set the default value to -1 so you can easily identify if a score wasn’t set correctly.

     







  2. Custom Widget Methods: Next, we define a getter method that allow us to retrieve the values of these properties within SAC.






  3. Model & Data Action: Now we just need a model in the SAC to store the game-related data from the widget and set up a data action to push the data into the model. We’ll use a basic model with relevant dimensions and measures to store game data.



  4. SAC Story (Optimized Design Experience)

 

Workflow overview

There are two main game modes:

  • Single Player: Play against a basic, rule-based AI bot (no fancy learning algorithms here, just a classic bot minding its own business).

  • Multiplayer: Play against a colleague on the same keyboard using simple controls like ‘W’ and ‘S’ for Player 1 and the ‘Up’ and ‘Down’ arrow keys for Player 2.

While it would have been possible to manage game IDs and players entirely within the custom widget, I chose to use standard elements to reduce widget complexity and test the integration capability. In the end, both ways lead to sufficient results.

But enough talk - A picture is worth a thousand words. A GIF even more. Let's illustrate the workflow with a practical example of the implementation:

Single Player

Multi Player

As you can see, the game flow is simple. We create players and a unique game ID. Afterwards, the game is played, and the results are stored via data action. In addition, I added a hierarchy to the game dimension, enabling us to group matches under parent nodes. This helps to distinguish between different game sessions - perfect for scenarios like playing a best-of-three series against a colleague.

Let’s quickly clarify, how the creation of transactional data works.
Like already mentioned, we dynamically update the custom widgets properties to store the goals each player achieves and derive which player won / lost the game. These properties can be read using custom methods we define in our custom widget like ‘getScore()’. Adding ‘console.log()’, we can display the game result in the console.

Now, we can use these values to set numeric parameters of a data action to persist the results in our model.

 

Reporting

Once the game is played and data is stored, you can easily build a report to visualize your stats. Of course, the possibilities are endless - this could be expanded to track win rates, visualizing seasons and tournaments or even predict game outcomes. But for now, we’ve kept it simple.

 

Extras

Alongside the core gameplay, I added a few extra touches with the custom widget:

  • Custom Themes: A custom theme can change the ball to a logo and change the color of different parts like the paddles.

  • Game Music: A theme melody plays in the background during the game, with sound effects for paddle collisions, a victory sound when you win, and a sad tone for losing.

  • Practice Mode: A mode that allows players to practice without storing any data.

 

Key Insights to Take Away:

  1. Custom Widgets Are Cool: The ability to create interactive, personalized widgets within SAP Analytics Cloud is an incredible feature. Custom widgets enable you to go beyond standard functionalities, like embedding games for a bit of fun.

  2. Bring Planning to Life: The integration of planning functionality with a game, like tracking your scores, demonstrates how SAC can blend analytics with custom interactive experiences.

  3. Creative Opportunities: The combination of SAC's customization and standard features allows for many use cases. Whether you are creating a game or a more complex business-related scenario, the toolset allows creativity.

Ready to give custom widgets a try? Dive in, and let the games begin! 😊 🎮

Cheers,

Tim

entry.title: SAP Fiori Application Generator Available in Open Source
entry.content:
The SAP Fiori tools team are delighted to announce that the underlying code for the SAP Fiori Application generator has been moved entirely to open source. This follows in our vision that was first outlined by our Chief Architect in a blog post from 2021 (Yes, this has been a long journey ‌🙂‌). 

Why Open Source?

Our goals haven’t changed—only expanded: 

  • Tighter collaboration. Work side-by-side with the community rather than behind closed doors. 
  • Full transparency. See exactly how the generator works, suggest improvements, or fork it for your own needs. 
  • Faster innovation & broader adoption. Community pull-requests beat private roadmaps every time. 

Our open source GitHub project now contains multiple packages that comprise the SAP Fiori Application Generator and span functionality from generation to deployment, including but not limited to: 

PackageWhat it does
@sap-ux/axios-extensionHandles authenticated HTTP calls so your generator prompts fetches remote data seamlessly. 

@SAP-ux/*-writer

Reusable file writers that output consistent and version-appropriate SAP Fiori application files for building SAP Fiori Elements and SAP Fiori Freestyle UI5 applications.
@sap-ux/deploy-toolingOpinionated deploy tasks for ABAP systems.
@sap-ux/odata-service-inquirer Provides reusable configurable inquirer prompts (for use in Yeoman generators) for connecting to multiple back-end types and retrieval of service data.
@sap-ux/fiori-app-sub-generator The main entry point for generation of SAP Fiori Elements based applications. Once a template type is selected in the @sap/generator-fiori generator this is the sub-generator that co-ordinates prompting the user, using the inquirers and generates the files, using the writers.

You’ll also find shared utilities, end-to-end test suites, and plenty of docs. 

 Image - SAP Fiori Tools and Generators - High-Level Modules and Dependencies

Not a simple "lift & shift"

Before open-sourcing the code, we broke up a monolith, added TypeScript typings, improved test coverage, and simplified internal APIs so you get clean, reusable, and well-documented code from day one.

How Can You help?

• Utilise our tools, try generating a SAP Fiori app, deploy it, and tell us where we can improve?
• Star & fork the repos of interest to raise their visibility.
• Open issues or PRs—bug fixes, new features, documentation tweaks.

Is there anything missing that you think should be there? Let us know - open a feature request at https://github.com/SAP/open-ux-tools/issues.

entry.title: Restrict additional LOIPRO IDOC through BADI
entry.content:
We are currently facing an issue in the production environment where a duplicate LOIPRO IDoc is being triggered each time the production order status is updated from MES. This duplicate LOIPRO IDoc is generated from SAP and sent to MES.

 

The business has requested to restrict the transmission of this duplicate IDoc to MES.

 

Upon analysis, we reviewed the DRF configuration and observed that no filters are currently maintained. We also explored the use of the standard BAdI IDOC_CREATION_CHECK, but during debugging of the production order, we found that this BAdI is not being triggered. As a result, we are unable to move forward with this approach.

 

Could you please review this scenario and share your insights or suggestions on how we can restrict the generation or transmission of the additional LOIPRO IDoc?

However, the client creates rework order with CO01. With rework orders, it is common to not have BOM. So my solution is to create production versions without BOM assignments for Rework scenario. However, after I create production version without BOM assignment, normal production order can choose production versions without BOM assignment. 

The client does not want the system to show production version without bom assignment

entry.title: Re-authentication screen issue while authenticating user
entry.content:
When trying to authenticate the user in the Re-authentication screen an Unauthorised user error received. What could be the issue?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Re-authentication screen issue while authenticating user
entry.content:
The issue is that the Re-Authentication screen is design to re-authenticate a user already previously authenticated (logged in). This is useful if you may want to enforce additional security when a user navigates to a sensitive area in the site...

If the user is logged out, won't be able to login using the re-authentication screen. This is because there is a difference is the loginMode parameter sent with the request.

In a default login process the loginMode is 'standard', which indicates to CDC that this user has not logged in at the moment, while in a re-authentication the loginMode parameter is sent with the 'reAuth' value, indicating the user is logged in at the moment, but as a security measure has to provide credentials again.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CUSTOMER_DATA_CLOUD/8b8d6fffe113457094a17701f63e3d6a/416fc0d470b21014bbc5a10ce4041860.html#re-authentication-flow

If the user session already expired the user has to login again, for that reason the user should be redirected to the login screen, not the re-authentication screen which is not designed for this purpose.

entry.title: Retrieve Data from Rest API to ABAP
entry.content:
Introduction

In abap, we create and expose API service through the OData gateway client for integration with third-party systems. This blog focuses on how to retrieve data from a REST API using a standard ABAP class. I have created the OData service using the GET_ENTITY method in an OData project and consumed that API using the approach described below.

  • cl_http_client
  • if_http_client
****Data Declarations****
TYPES: BEGIN OF tt_json,
         MaterialCode TYPE matnr,           "Material
         UserName     TYPE ernam,           "Creator Name
         mtart        TYPE mtart,           "Material Type
       END OF tt_json.

TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_field,
         fielname TYPE char40,              "Field Name
       END OF ty_field.

DATA: lt_material     TYPE TABLE OF tt_json,
      lt_tab          TYPE TABLE OF ty_field,
      ls_material     TYPE tt_json,
      lo_http         TYPE REF TO if_http_client,    "HTTP Client Abstraction
      lr_data         TYPE REF TO data,
      lv_result       TYPE string,
      lv_content_type TYPE string,
      lv_action_type  TYPE string.

FIELD-SYMBOLS: <ls_data>   TYPE data,
               <ls_d>      TYPE data,
               <ls_struct> TYPE data,
               <lv_any>    TYPE any,
               <lv_char>.

SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK a1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-002.
  PARAMETERS: p_mat TYPE matnr OBLIGATORY.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK a1.

*Get URL
DATA(lv_url) =  |{ TEXT-001 }'{ p_mat }')|  .

The API and selection screen look likes,

 

 

"To perform the HTTP communication using factory method.
cl_http_client=>create_by_url(
  EXPORTING
    url                    = lv_url           " URL
  IMPORTING
    client                 = lo_http          " HTTP Client Abstraction
  EXCEPTIONS
    argument_not_found     = 1                " Communication parameter (host or service) not available
    plugin_not_active      = 2                " HTTP/HTTPS communication not available
    internal_error         = 3                " Internal error (e.g. name too long)
    OTHERS                 = 7
).
"
IF sy-subrc = 0.
  lv_content_type  = 'application/json'.
  lv_action_type   = 'GET'.
  lo_http->request->set_content_type(  lv_content_type ).
  lo_http->request->set_method( lv_action_type ).
"The output shows a json format.
  lo_http->request->set_header_field(
  EXPORTING
    name  =  'Accept'                          " Name of the header field
    value =  'application/json'                " HTTP header field value
).
"To ignore the authentication during execution.
  lo_http->propertytype_logon_popup = if_http_client=>co_disabled.
  lo_http->authenticate(
  EXPORTING
    proxy_authentication = ' '            " Proxy Logon (= 'X')
    client               = '800'          " R/3 system (client number from logon)
    username             = 'username'     " ABAP System, User Logon Name
    password             = 'password'     " Logon ID
    language             = 'E'            " SAP System, Current Language
).
ENDIF.

lo_http->send(
  EXPORTING
    timeout                    = 15                 " Timeout of Answer Waiting Time
  EXCEPTIONS
    http_communication_failure = 1                  " Communication Error
    http_invalid_state         = 2                  " Invalid state
    http_processing_failed     = 3                  " Error when processing method
    http_invalid_timeout       = 4                  " Invalid Time Entry
    OTHERS                     = 5
).
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
*   WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.

lo_http->receive(
  EXCEPTIONS
    http_communication_failure = 1                " Communication Error
    http_invalid_state         = 2                " Invalid state
    http_processing_failed     = 3                " Error when processing method
    OTHERS                     = 4
).
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
*   WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.

*Get the response from target as character string cdata( ).
lv_result = lo_http->response->get_cdata( ).
lo_http->close( ).

We have the response from target system and need to retrieve the data from the string variable lv_result.

 

*Deserialize the data from Json to Abap
/ui2/cl_json=>deserialize(
  EXPORTING
    json             =  lv_result         " JSON string
  CHANGING
    data             =  lr_data           " Data to serialize
).

lt_tab =  VALUE #( ( fielname = 'MATERIALCODE')
                   ( fielname = 'USERNAME')
                   ( fielname = 'MTART'  ) ).

ASSIGN lr_data->* TO <ls_data>.
ASSIGN COMPONENT 'd' OF STRUCTURE <ls_data> TO <ls_d>.

DATA(lo_typedescr) = cl_abap_typedescr=>describe_by_data( <ls_d> ).
ASSIGN <ls_d>->* TO <ls_struct>.
IF <ls_struct> IS ASSIGNED.
  LOOP AT lt_tab INTO DATA(ls_tab).
    ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_tab-fielname OF STRUCTURE <ls_struct> TO <lv_any>.
    IF <lv_any> IS ASSIGNED.
      ASSIGN <lv_any>->* TO <lv_char>.
      IF <lv_char> IS ASSIGNED.
        ASSIGN COMPONENT ls_tab-fielname OF STRUCTURE ls_material TO FIELD-SYMBOL(<lv_target>).
        IF <lv_target> IS ASSIGNED.
          <lv_target> = <lv_char>.
        ENDIF.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.
UNASSIGN: <ls_data>,<ls_d>,<ls_struct>,<lv_any>,<lv_char>,<lv_target>.

cl_demo_output=>begin_section( 'Material Detail' ).
cl_demo_output=>write_data( ls_material ).
cl_demo_output=>next_section( 'OData Service URL' ).
cl_demo_output=>write_text( lv_url ).
cl_demo_output=>display( ).
CLEAR: ls_material.

The output should be like below in debugger level.

 

Test the above service URI to postman app in GET method.

Note: In the example above, I retrieved data from an OData HTTP API that does not require an SSL certificate. However, when communication with external APIs. an SSL certificate is typically required. We will cover this in detail in a separate blog post.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: PO reference in Serialization report
entry.content:
Hi Sathyan,

In IQ03/IQ09, if you enter the material and serial number and press the History button, you should see a line for the assigned purchase order item. If this doesn't work, please create a support ticket.

IE03 will not work, if no full equipment was created for the serial number. This could either be done by setting Equipment Requirement in the Serial Number Profile to 02 (always with equipment) or by manually creating the equipment view. But it is not needed if you just want to trace serial numbers in logistics processes.

BR,
Alex

entry.title: Pass Data Action Parameter Value Directly from Dropdowns through Script in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud allows you to create interactive dashboards and automate actions using scripting. A common requirement is to pass values selected in dropdowns directly into a Data Action. This guide walks through how to link dropdown inputs to Data Action parameters using scripting.

Step 1: Create the Data Action

  • Go to the Main Menu and select Data Action.
  • Create a new Data Action.
  • Define your dynamic parameters.
  • Add your required calculations or steps using those parameters.

Example: Let’s assume your Data Action uses 1 measure and 1 dimension.

Step 2: Set Up the Story

  • Open your story from the menu bar or create a new one.
  • Insert a Table and two Dropdowns.
    (Refer to our earlier blog if you need help creating dropdowns. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/interlinked-dropdown-filters-using-script-in-sap-analytics-cloud-sac-story/ba-p/14103275 )

Step 3: Set Up the Data Action in the Story

  1. In the left-side panel, open the Scripting section.
  2. Click the + and choose Data Action.
  3. Name your data action (e.g., DataAction_1).

     4. In the Detail section, assign the data action you created earlier.

Step 4: Add a Trigger Button

  1. Go to the Insert menu and add a Button to your story.
  2. Select the Button, then open the Script Editor.
  3. In the onClick() event, use the following script as an example:
var pVersion=Dropdown_1.getSelectedKey();
var pMeasure=Dropdown_2.getSelectedKey();

DataAction_1.setParameterValue("TargetVersion",pVersion);
DataAction_1.setParameterValue("measure1",pMeasure);

if(DataAction_1.execute()){
Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Success,"DA run succesfully");
Application.refreshData();}
  • Here, Targetversion and measure1 are the parameter names defined in your Data Action.
  • Dropdown_1 and Dropdown_2 are the IDs of your dropdown widgets.

Step 5: View Mode and Execution

  • Switch to View Mode.
  • Use the dropdowns to filter or select values.
  • Click the Button.
  • The Data Action will execute with the parameter values fetched directly from the dropdowns.

Conclusion

By using scripting in SAC stories, you can efficiently pass dropdown values into Data Actions. This approach makes your dashboards dynamic and user-driven, enabling better interactivity and automation.

This pattern is ideal for what-if scenarios, scenario planning, or on-the-fly calculations based on user inputs.

Try adapting it to your own use cases and streamline the planning experience!

entry.title: Reflections from SAP Sapphire 2025 – What really stood out
entry.content:
Let’s start with what was new

The biggest headline? SAP is going all-in on Business AI. CEO Christian Klein introduced the concept of the “Business AI Flywheel” – the idea being: when you combine SAP’s applications, rich business data, and AI capabilities, you get real momentum. It’s not about throwing AI into the mix for the sake of it. It’s about embedding it meaningfully into real processes.

A key part of that vision is Joule, SAP’s AI copilot. It’s no longer a beta feature in the background – it’s now embedded across the suite, supporting over 1,600 business scenarios. I tested a few of them during the event. Some are genuinely helpful – think productivity boosters in procurement or real-time suggestions in finance – while others still need polish. But the direction is promising.

Tech that’s moving fast

Another interesting piece is the AI Foundation, which brings all SAP AI components under one roof. One thing I particularly liked: the Prompt Optimizer. It helps generate the right prompts for AI use cases, which is great because – let’s be honest – not everyone is a prompt engineer.

SAP is also doubling down on its Business Data Cloud. This is more than just a data lake. They’re working with partners like Palantir to let businesses connect and analyze SAP and non-SAP data in real time, with a common semantic layer. In plain English: it’s becoming easier to get your data to actually work for you.

Where things are headed

If there’s one message SAP hammered home, it’s this: Clean Core is non-negotiable. That means no more heavy modifications inside your ERP system. Instead, extensions should run on the SAP BTP. It’s a good move for system stability – but it also means companies need to rethink how they’ve built things in the past. As consultants, we’re going to spend a lot more time guiding clients through that shift.

Also worth noting: SAP is getting serious about Agentic AI – think of AI “agents” that make decisions and take actions in areas like supply chain or sales. It’s still early days, but the use cases are already being prototyped.

But not everything is simple

Now, let’s be real: a lot of what SAP showed is cloud-only. For companies still running on-premise, this creates friction. You can’t just turn on the new AI stuff unless you're in the cloud or on a RISE/GROW path. That’s a tough pill for some customers.

And while SAP is talking about 30% productivity gains with AI – that doesn’t just happen by installing a new feature. You need the right processes, user training, and change management. Otherwise, the tech won’t stick.

Final thoughts

Sapphire 2025 was inspiring – not because of flashy demos, but because SAP seems to have found a clear focus: helping businesses become truly intelligent, data-driven, and adaptive. But let’s not kid ourselves: the road there takes effort. Clean core isn’t just a buzzword. It’s a shift in how we design and run enterprise systems.

For me as a consultant, this was a reminder: our role is more critical than ever. Clients need guidance – not just on what’s possible, but on what’s practical. And that’s where we can make the difference.

entry.title: How To Implement Alert Notification Service in Cloud Application Programming Model (CAPM) Project
entry.content:
Step 1: Go to Subaccount -> Go To Instance and Subscription -> Click Create Button

Step 2: In Service Select Alert Notification and In the Instance Name Section Give Any Name, Leave Other Fields as It Is.

Note: If you did not create any space then you see create space option just below space filed. Just click on that give space name and choose roles and click Create

Step 3: Access SAP Alert Notification Cockpit and Create a Subscription

  1. After creating an instance, it will be listed under Instance and Subscription.

  2. Click on the instance name from the list.

  3. This will open the SAP Alert Notification Cockpit in a new page.

  4. In the cockpit, navigate to the Subscription section.

  5. Click the Create button to start creating a new subscription.

Step 4: Create Subscription with Condition and Action

  1. Create Subscription:

    • Go to the subscription creation section.

    • Enter a random name for the subscription.

    • Click Create to proceed to the next step.

  2. Create Condition:

    • Enter a random name for the condition.

    • In the Condition section, choose the criteria for triggering the email.

      • Example: Set eventType to Contains → Warning.

    • Click Create.

    • If multiple conditions are created, you can select the relevant one in the next step.

    • Click Save and Next to proceed.

  3. Create Action:

    • Select Email as the action type.

    • Click Next.

    • Provide:

      • Action Name

      • Email Address of the recipient.

      • Subject Template (for email header)

      • Payload Template (for email body)

    • Click Create.

    • On the next page, select the appropriate action and click Save.

Notes:

  • Note 1: You can create Actions and Conditions separately by navigating directly to the Action or Condition tabs from the left-side menu.

  • Note 2: The recipient email address will receive a confirmation email. They must accept it to start receiving notifications.

  • Note 3: To send Email Subject and Body dynamically from your application payload, leave the Subject Template and Payload Template fields blank.

Step 5: Create a Service Key for the Alert Notification Instance

  1. After setting up the Alert Notification instance, locate it in the list.

  2. Click on the arrow ( > ) next to the instance name.

  3. A panel will open on the right side of the screen.

  4. In the right panel, look for the Service Key section.

  5. Click the Create button.

  6. A popup window will appear for creating a new service key.

  7. Enter a name for the service key.

  8. Click Create to generate the key.

 

 

Step 6: Create Destination for Use in CAPM Application

  1. After creating the service key, the next step is to create a destination.

  2. This destination will be used in your CAPM application.

  3. To get the required details (URL, Client ID, Client Secret):

    • Click on the instance.

    • Select View Credentials to open the service key details.

    • Copy the URL, Client ID, and Client Secret from the credentials.

  4. In the left-side panel, go to Connectivity → Destinations.

  5. Click the Create Destination button.

  6. Fill in the destination details as shown in the reference screenshot:

    • URL

    • Authentication method

    • Client ID

    • Client Secret

    • Any other required fields

  7. Click Create to save the destination.

 

Step 7: Integrate Alert Notification in a CAPM Application

  1. Open or create a CAPM application where you want to integrate the alert notification.

  2. In the CAPM application, set up a connection to the destination you created earlier.

  3. Use the standard API endpoint to trigger the notification:

     
    /cf/producer/v1/resource-events

  4. This endpoint allows your application to send events to the SAP Alert Notification service.

  5. For full API details and supported parameters, refer to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub

 
 
 

Step 8: Test Using Postman

  1. Deploy your service.

  2. Use the deployed service URL.

  3. Get the Client ID and Client Secret from the environment variable under the xsuaa section and use it in postman for authentication using oAuth 2.0.

 
 

Conclusion:

By following the above steps, we have successfully set up and integrated SAP Alert Notification with a CAPM application. Starting from creating the alert notification instance, setting up conditions and actions, generating a service key, creating a destination, and finally integrating and testing the service using Postman — the entire process ensures that your application can send real-time alerts based on defined events. This integration enhances monitoring and improves responsiveness to critical events in your system.

Hi Dominik,

I read carefully this note, but this is for the calculation of the GR /IR during an invoice posting. For the invoice, the MENGE field refers indeed to the quantity that is entered in the current invoice that you are posting.

In my case, I am in the calculation of the GR/IR posting during the reversal of a MR11 posting. There is no invoice that is currently being posted. In the reversal process, you just select an MR11 posting and click on "reverse". There is no quantity that you entered during the MR11SHOW transaction. That's why I don't understand this MENGE quantity.

entry.title: Accelerate SAP Commerce Cloud application development with AI coding assistants
entry.content:
One of the significant challenges faced with meeting the development of ecommerce applications is to hire qualified candidates, onboard them quickly and retain them for the required amount of time. Any unexpected attrition during the project lifetime impacts the planning and timelines.

SAP Commerce Cloud comes out-of-box with a readily available code base and developers work on top of the same to customize and enhance as per business needs. The main goals involved in SAP Commerce Coud project implementations are to write new code, understand existing code, enhance code, write tests, fix defects, debug code, document code etc., Developers use IDES like IntelliJ, Eclipse, Visual Studio etc., to work on coding using Java, angular etc., and routinely perform these repetitive tasks on day-to-day basis. Less experienced developers often look for guidance from their seniors and rely on search engines like Google or programming question-and-answer sites like Stack Overflow for writing or understanding the code. The code quality, time taken, efforts spent differ from developer to developer because of absence of standard development approaches.

AI coding assistant tools address these challenges as they are designed to help write code, review, debug and optimize code. Their intended purpose is to assist with software development duties making the coding process easier. These tools use artificial intelligence to help developers with real-time code suggestions, auto-completions, code snippets based on natural language comments or questions. This saves time, reduces errors, suggests best practices, helps learn new coding techniques. The available tools in the market provide simple plugin integrations with IDEs usually used by ecommerce developers. The developer wouldn’t need any more to open a browser and search for guidance and would instead get suggestions within the IDE where the code is being worked upon. Few examples of popular coding assistants in the market are Github Copilot, Amazon Code Whisperer, JetBrains AI assistant etc.,

Of the various AI coding assistants, Github Copilot shows quite promising in making software development life cycle workflows easier in SAP Commerce Cloud projects. It plays the role of an AI pair programmer helping developers to code faster with less effort. It integrates as a plugin installing into an IDE which populates multiple autosuggestions of code which can be picked and used by the developer. It also comes with a Github Copilot Chat giving code suggestions inline as one type’s. Copilot Chat gives much richer pair programming experience right in the editor. Help can be asked while coding right where code is being written. Some examples of conversing in the chat by selecting a piece of code are as below

“Explain the selected code”

“Make this code more readable”

“Propose a fix for the bug in this code”

“Write a set of unit tests”

By adopting the AI coding assistant usage, developers can be relieved of the heavy lifting and can focus on developing business code instead of writing glue code. Developers using AI-based tools will get equipped to be at their best productive and can significantly improve their developer experience. Developers can also focus on creative tasks like designing, brainstorming, collaborating and planning.

Choosing the right AI coding assistant tool, which is committed to data privacy and security, creating responsible AI by design, would benefit development of SAP Commerce Cloud application in terms of release cycle agility, developer productivity and better code quality.

entry.title: Inquiry about automatic retrieval/export of payment files from SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear Support Team,

 

We are currently working on an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation 

 

We have noticed that when generating payment files using the "Manage Payment Media" app, it is unclear whether these files are stored in any accessible repository within SAP, or if there is any API, service, or mechanism available to automatically retrieve them from an external system.

 

Specifically, we would like to understand:

Are the files generated by the Manage Payment Media app stored in any repository within the system that can be accessed via standard services?

Is it possible to export or automatically retrieve these files (e.g., via integration or API) to an external system?

 

 

This is important because, in the current SAP R/3 system, Criteria Caixa automatically retrieves these files and stores them in an external system for further processing.

 

We would appreciate your guidance on the standard capabilities of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud in this regard, and any recommended alternatives.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Inquiry about automatic retrieval/export of payment f...
entry.content:
The file download from Fiori apps triggers browser download, so file path depends on each user's browser setting.

  • There isn't a configuration option to automatically save the payment media file to a specific server path within the cloud environment.
  • The standard way to access the completed payment media file is by manually downloading it via the "Manage Payment Media" app.   
  • For automated retrieval and storage in a specific external location, you need to implement the "Application Interface to Retrieve Payment Files" (Scope Item 2YM).

"Manage Payment Media" App (F1868): This is the primary SAP Fiori app used to work with payment media. You can use this app to:

  • View existing payment media with their processing status.   

  • Download the generated payment media files manually. When you download, the file will be saved to the default download location configured in your web browser settings. The file will typically be in a .txt or .xml format, depending on the payment medium format

Automate File Retrieval and Transfer:
Implement the "Application Interface to Retrieve Payment Files" (Scope Item 2YM), which provides a SOAP service to programmatically retrieve payment medium files from SAP S/4HANA Cloud. This allows external systems to fetch these files for further processing.

Transfer Files to Desired Location:
Utilize middleware solutions, such as SAP Integration Suite (formerly SAP CPI), to facilitate the transfer of payment files from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to your desired local or network location. This middleware can handle protocols like SFTP to securely move files to specified directories.

Hi Sandra,

we discussed this in our team and we agree to the described view. The removal of the do-not-show-again option for the '>30 breakpoint error popup' is planned for the August update of ADT.

If a user double clicks a line that contains no executable ABAP code (e.g. an empty line or a line with a variable declaration) then the current behavior will be unchanged and the do-not-show-again option will still be available.

Thanks for your input and I hope you did not loose too much time when you had to figure out why it was impossible to create another breakpoint. I guess that was an annoying search.

Best regards,
Armin

entry.title: Re: Final settlement is not considering the previous sett...
entry.content:
Hello,

Steps to Resolve Multi-Payee Condition Contract Settlement Collect Settlement Documents - Condition Contracts Issues

  1. Review Condition Contract Settings:

    • Ensure that you have correctly defined the condition contract type and its settings. The contract type affects how business volumes are determined and how settlements are processed.

  2. Check Settlement Calendar Entries:

    • Verify that all previous settlements have been entered correctly in the settlement calendar. Ensure that the execution dates for these settlements are aligned with the final settlement requirements.

  3. Verify Business Volume Selection Criteria:

    • Double-check the business volume selection criteria to confirm all relevant transactions have been included. This can affect the calculation of the settlement amount.

  4. Analyze Settlement Data:

    • Review the settlement type and ensure it is set to handle accounts payable or receivable correctly, depending on your business process.

  5. Review Condition Types:

    • Look into the condition types set for these contracts. Incorrect settings here can lead to improper calculations.

  6. Use Supported Applications:

    • Use SAPâ€™s applications designed for settlement management to ensure you are executing settlements correctly, like Collect Settlement Documents - Condition Contracts for collective processing.

Useful Tips

  • Logging and Monitoring: It’s important to use logging tools to identify any discrepancies in processing previous settlements.
  • Additional Settlements: If previous settlements need adjustment, this should be handled before initiating final settlement.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

The Undo action is available only on Q1.0 engine.

entry.title: Error "Duplicate column names in the result" with columns from different tables (BigQuery DB)
entry.content:
Hello,

On WebI, I get an error when a query contains 2 or more columns with the same name but from different tables.

For example, in the following query:

SELECT
TABLE_1.COL_NAME
TABLE_2.COL_NAME
(...)

=> TABLE_1.COL_NAME and TABLE_2.COL_NAME are interpreted as duplicate coloumn names. I get the following error and the query cannot be executed either on Webi or the IDT query panel:

"The following database error occurred: [Simba][BigQuery] (70) Invalid query: Duplicate column names in the result are not supported when a destination table is present. Found duplicate(s): COL_NAME.
For information about this error, please refer to SAP Knowledge Base Article 2054721 on the SAP Support Portal. (IES 10901)"

There is no error when I run the query directly on BigQuery.

Environnement info:
BO version: 4.3 SP4 Patch 6
connection: native ODBC BigQuery driver

As a workaround, I add an alias in the SQL definition of the objets in the universe. Unfortunatly, this cannot be a sustainable solution.


Note: The problem did not occur on our previous platform BO 4.2 SP9 Patch 7 using a generic ODBC driver (which is not supported by SAP).

Thank you in advance

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh Blog Collection
entry.content:
Introduction

In today’s fast-paced, event-driven world, seamless data movement and real-time responsiveness have become critical to modern application architectures. SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) offers a powerful solution—enabling dynamic, reliable, and scalable event streaming across hybrid and multi-cloud environments.

Over time, we've created a substantial amount of content around Advanced Event Mesh—now it makes sense to bring all the pieces together in one place as a cohesive blog collection, including Guides & contributions from other AEM Experts in the Community.

This blog serves as a central hub—a curated collection of insightful articles, technical deep dives and best practices focused on AEM and SAP Event Enablement Options like RAP, AIFAEM and the SAP Event-Enablement Add-On (ASAPIO). Whether you're just getting started or looking to optimize a production-grade deployment, this collection is designed to guide you through the concepts and capabilities of AEM.

Let’s explore how event mesh technology is transforming integration strategies and empowering developers and architects to build the next generation of event-driven systems.

 

Getting Started

Get Started with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh

Advanced Event Mesh: Basic Concepts 

Advanced Event Mesh: Create your first event broker

 

AEM Features

SAP Advanced Event Mesh – Manage Advance Event Mesh with API’s 

Automating Advanced Event Mesh Broker Creation 

Advanced Event Mesh: Replay

Advanced Event Mesh: Queue 

Enabling In-Order processing with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh Partitioned Queues

Securing SAP Advanced Event Mesh: OAuth Configuration and SEMP API Integration

 

Connectivity & Auth

Advanced Event Mesh: Client Authentication - Basic / OAuth 

Advanced Event Mesh Connectors and Easy Event-Driven Example of S/4HANA with Amazon S3 Integration

 

Event Portal / Developer Hub

Enable SAP Developer Hub as central Event Catalog

Discover S/4HANA Cloud Events in SAP Developer Hub

 

Monitoring

Configure SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh Distributed Tracing

Evaluate SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh Datadog Distributed Tracing

Advanced Event Mesh:Troubleshooting

 

User Management

Introduction to Group Management in SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh

Streamlining User Management in Advanced Event Mesh with SAP CIS and Identity Federation

SAP Advanced Event Mesh – Event Access Approval Workflow 

 

Deployment Options

Setting Up a Private SAP Advanced Event Mesh Cluster on SUSE Rancher RKE2

 

Kafka Bridge

SAP Integration Suite – Connect Everything FEATURING Kafka

Bridging Advanced Event Mesh and Kafka: Dynamic Topics and Headers in Action

 

SAP Event Enablement

RAP

RAP Business Events with SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh and SAP S/4HANA On Premise 2023

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [1]: Connecting AEM to S/4HANA On Premise 2023

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [2]: Creating custom business events

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [3]: Creating events with deep payloads

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [4]: Creating a derived event

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [5]: Consuming a local business event

RAP Business Events with Advanced Event Mesh [6]: Consuming a remote event

 

AIFAEM

A Natural Choice: SAP AIF can now expose events to Advanced Event Mesh to power your EDA 

How to set up enhanced Events for Advanced Event Mesh with Application Interface Framework & AIFAEM

How to send out IDocs to Advanced Event Mesh using SAP Application Interface Framework & AIFAEM

How to set up Filters in AIFAEM

How to Display the JSON Payload in AIFAEM

How to send Custom HTTP Headers in AIFAEM

 

Initial Loads

Blog Series - Intitial Loads to SAP Advanced Event Mesh

 

Other

BTP Event Mesh Advance Concepts

 

Summary

This collection brings together key insights and technical deep dives around Advanced Event Mesh to help you navigate, implement, and optimize event-driven architectures. If you notice that a relevant blog post or important topic is missing, feel free to let me know in the comments—I'm happy to expand the list and keep this collection as complete and useful as possible.

entry.title: ABAP News for SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2505
entry.content:
In this blog, you'll learn about the new features for SAP BTP ABAP Environment 2505 that were documented in the ABAP keyword documentation and ABAP data models documentation.

News for ABAP Release 9.15 

ABAP CDS

CDS Delegated Buffers

Delegated buffers in CDS are view entity buffers that delegate the buffer usage to the buffer of the base object. This saves memory in the AS ABAP. To turn buffer delegation on or off, use the annotation:

@AbapCatalog.entityBuffer.definitionType:[#DELEGATED]

To define a delegated buffer, use the syntax:

DEFINE VIEW ENTITY BUFFER ON cds_entity … TYPE DELEGATED

More about CDS delegated buffers

ABAP SQL

Level-based Hierarchies

The hierarchy function in ABAP SQL can now create an ABAP SQL hierarchy as a level-based hierarchy. Level-based hierarchies allow access to hierarchical data in data sources defined by level columns. To define a level-based hierarchy, use the addition LEVELS:

... HIERARCHY( SOURCE data_source ...
               LEVELS ( col1, col2,... )
               ... ) ...

More about level-based hierarchies

ABAP Keyword Documentation

Starting with this release, we will publish the ABAP Keyword Documentation content with the UI5 design. With the UI5 design, the documentation is even more aligned with SAP's corporate design. The functionality of the ADT design remains, and you can still search with the type-ahead functionality and send links. In addition, you can now copy code snippets and switch between syntax variants by clicking on tabs. In the header section, you can click the News button to know what's new in the current release or switch to an earlier one.

More about the UI5 design

Test and Consume CDS Data Models

The ABAP Data Models documentation is the home of two new sections: Test and Consume.

  • In Test, you find information about how to develop automated tests for CDS entities. Currently, unit tests for CDS entities are covered, using the ABAP CDS test double framework. Additionally, the AMDP test double framework has been integrated into ABAP for unit testing CDS table functions.
  • In Consume, you find information on the local consumption and service consumption of CDS data models.

More about Test
More about Consume

New Terms

The following new terms are used.

TermDefinition
RAP event-driven side effectA RAP side effect that is triggered by a business event for side effects. The business event in question is defined to be used exclusively for a side effect with the syntax event MyEvent for side effects.
RAP change documents

An optional part of an entity behavior definition that enables change logging of persisted RAP BO data for a change documents-enabled RAP BO entity. When enabled, changes are written to a change document object. RAP change documents are a RAP reuse service.
CDS table entity
 
A CDS persistent entity that defines a database table in ABAP CDS. A table entity is defined as a CDS data definition using the statement DEFINE TABLE ENTITY.
writable database objectA Repository object that allows modifying data on the database.
  • In ABAP CDS, CDS table entities, writable CDS view entities, and writable CDS external entities allow for the modification of data with the ABAP SQL statements INSERT, UPDATE, MODIFY, and DELETE.
  • In ABAP Dictionary, DDIC database tables allow data change operations.
writable CDS view entityA CDS view entity that allows modifying data on the database using the ABAP SQL INSERT, UPDATE, MODIFY, and DELETE operations. Defined using the statement WRITABLE.
writable CDS external entityA dynamic external entity that allows modifying data on an external database system using the ABAP SQL INSERT, UPDATE, MODIFY, and DELETE operations. Defined using the statement WRITABLE in the CDS data definition. An ABAP SQL connection object is required to establish a service connection and enable the logical external schema for write access.
ABAP SQL connection objectAn  instance of a class that implements IF_ABAP_SQL_CONNECTION and represents a valid database connection.
level-based hierarchyAn  ABAP SQL hierarchy that is generated from a SQL data source where certain columns define from left to right the hierarchy levels and where each row content of these columns represents a path from a start node to a leaf node that spans these levels. In a level-based hierarchy, all leaf nodes have the same level that is the highest level defined in the data source. For each leaf node, a similar set of ancestor nodes is generated for the lower levels of the data source. In ABAP SQL, the addition LEVELS of the hierarchy generator HIERARCHY can be used to generate level-based hierarchies.
parent-child hierarchyA hierarchy, where the hierarchy edges are parent-child relationships.

If you have any questions, feel free to comment below. We hope you enjoy the new features.

entry.title: Work Order and Notification Required Start/End Dates not synchronized according to Priority config
entry.content:
Priority type is configured and assigned to the Work Order and Notification.  When I created an emergency work order where the notification is auto-generated, I noticed a discrepancy in the Required Start and end date of the 2 documents.  Example P1 - 1 to 7 days ( config - Rel Start =1; Rel End = 7;  Unit = day).  

In Work Order:  Required Start = 05/28/2025 (mm-dd-yyyy); Required End = 06/05/2025 (mm-dd-yyyy)

In Notification: Require Start = 05/27/2025 ; Required End = 06/02/2025

In summary, the Notification dates are correct because the range is 7 days and it started on the date of creation itself. Whereas the Work Order required start is on the following day.

entry.title: Taking Flight with SAP: Marco's Journey to SAP Champion
entry.content:
“In a world that is constantly reinventing itself, I enjoy collecting and passing on knowledge and always thinking one step ahead.”  That mindset has guided Marco Büscher from his early days supporting flight operations in the military to becoming a highly skilled SAP consultant and solution architect.  

Over the past 15 years, his drive to understand how various business processes fit together has led him to master multiple SAP solutions. What started as a curiosity has evolved into expertise, and Marco now thrives in the dynamic world of business informatics, constantly learning and growing, both as a professional and a mentor. 

Marco’s SAP journey began after his time in the German Armed Forces, where he gained invaluable insights into flight operations. It was there he realized the importance of understanding complex systems from all angles, a lesson he later applied to his work with SAP software. He first encountered SAP solutions in his professional life as a business IT specialist, quickly recognizing how the system’s many modules could integrate to manage business processes effectively. 

A Personal Learning Sabbatical 

Over the years, Marco’s curiosity pushed him to continually expand his knowledge. “I received my first certification in 2009, when SAP S/4HANA was still a long way off,” he recalls. “Over the years, SAP has reinvented itself again and again”. To keep up with the evolving technology, he decided to take what he calls a “learning sabbatical”. He used this time to focus on deepening his expertise in SAP solutions and acquiring multiple SAP Certifications in ABAP and SAP S/4HANA solutions.  

“My certifications form the basis for my work as a consultant and technical architect at NTT DATA Deutschland SE,” he explains. “I utilize this experience to lead the way as a pioneer, provide guidance as a navigator and offer promising solutions as a designer in a complex and ever-changing landscape”.  

Marco credits SAP Learning Hub for providing clear, well-structured, guided learning resources that helped him to get and stay certified: “The learning journey was the best way for me to learn SAP,” he adds. From his perspective, upskilling and training is the only way to unlock the full potential of SAP software. 

"By acquiring knowledge in all key SAP solutions and combining it with my understanding of the system from a developer’s perspective, I am now able to satisfy customer requirements in both standard and customized SAP solutions”. 

A Leader and Mentor in the SAP Community 

Marco’s journey didn’t stop at mastering the technical aspects of SAP. Today, he is not only an expert in his field but also a mentor, sharing his knowledge and experiences with others who are looking to build their careers in SAP.  Through his active involvement in the SAP Community, particularly his participation in SAP CodeJams, he has become recognized as an SAP Champion, a role that fills him with pride.  

As he continues to guide others, he’s not just solving complex problems—he’s helping them see the bigger picture and empowering them to chart their own success in SAP. 

The Power of Continuous Learning 

Marco’s story is a testament to the power of continuous learning and self-improvement. His curiosity, commitment, and the willingness to learn new things has led him to embrace the full potential of SAP and seek out opportunities for growth. In doing so, Marco has not only shaped his own career but is also making a lasting impact on others, as is the goal of every SAP Champion. 

Accelerate your Career Today 

You too can benefit from tailored and self-paced learning opportunities that meet you when and where you learn best on SAP Learning site. Get certified to demonstrate your expertise and boost your career.

entry.title: Re: Interview Scheduling for Mass Recruitment
entry.content:
Hi @Kahley ,

Below are some more targeted suggestions and workarounds specifically for SuccessFactors Recruiting – Interview Scheduling for mass hiring.

1. Review the Interview Scheduling URL Format
Sometimes the issue may be due to malformed or incomplete links generated for certain candidates.

Validate the email template tokens: Go to Admin Center > Manage Recruiting Email Templates and confirm the correct tokens are being used for the interview scheduling link (e.g., [[INTERVIEW_SCHEDULING_URL]] or similar).

2. Use the “Interview Central” Alternative
Instead of mass emailing via the standard interview invitation, you can try a workaround:

Use Interview Central to manually assign slots if the system isn't handling mass scheduling well.

If automation is a must, consider splitting candidate lists into smaller batches (e.g., 10 at a time) — SuccessFactors sometimes has issues processing bulk updates or notifications.

3. Use “Interview Scheduling Assistant” Feature
This is a newer tool designed to improve scheduling.

Go to Admin Center > Manage Recruiting Settings, and ensure the Interview Scheduling Assistant is enabled.

It provides improved calendar views, integration with Microsoft Outlook/Google Calendar, and sometimes resolves page loading issues.

4. Check SuccessFactors Integration with MS Outlook / Google Calendar
Many "blank page" errors stem from integration or permission issues:

Ensure ICS file generation is functioning and that linked calendar systems (e.g., Office 365 or Google Workspace) are correctly integrated and authenticated in Admin Center > Configure Interview Scheduling.

Check if the interviewers' calendars are shared properly. If the system can’t access them, it may generate a broken or blank scheduling page.

Since you already submitted a ticket:

Escalate the ticket by referencing the number of affected candidates and hiring deadlines.

Attach screenshots, HAR files, and test links from affected candidates.

Request replication support — SAP may offer temporary hotfixes for known issues, especially if they can reproduce it in your instance.

Hope this helps!!

Thanks and Regards,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: ODP MODIFY DELTA DATE CLIENT EXTRACTOR
entry.content:
Hi

 

It is not supported to make manual changes in any administration tables. Especially not in tables that handles the delta pointer.

ODP supports the repeat of the last delta (just like the normal SAPI), yet there is no standard way to manipulate the subscriptions and they should not be touched at all.

If you need to load a specific data set, use a full load with a specific filter on the neede data.

 

br

Roland

entry.title: Output File Name changed in upper case automatically for program FAGL_CL_REGROUP
entry.content:
Hello,

 

We want to define Output File Name on program FAGL_CL_REGROUP, but the location path entered in lower case is automatically changed to upper case.

 

The problem is that upper case location is not readable on linux OS.

 

So instead having that : /apps/K_NICE/userdata/testspool

 

we have this location: /APPS/K_NICE/USERDATA/TESTSPOOL

entry.title: Discrepancy Analysis of Cross-currency Balances
entry.content:
First, open the application named "Display Customer Summary China".

 

Next, enter the query conditions to check the balance for December 2024, and it shows that the USD balance is 0.

 

Then, enter the query conditions again to look for the data of January 2025, and the initial balance of USD is displayed as 51.06.

 

 
It can be seen from the above that the balance at the end of December 2024 is inconsistent with the balance at the beginning of January 2025. After further analysis, it is found that the discrepancy is caused by the clearing of accounts between USD and CNY. However, this type of business is a normal operation. The problem is that the current report system is not capable of querying transaction currency balances across different currencies.

entry.title: Which Client to operate "SAP Access Control for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition in", 000 or ?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

this is a question about Client 000 and SAP Access Control for S/4HANA Private Edition.

Introduction & Background:

SAP Access Control for S/4HANA Private Edition documentation is here: 

SAP Access Control for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition 

and this documentation points to

-> Digital Library

which points to

-> SAP Access Control | SAP Help Portal

I don't see any recommendation or best practice in the documentation regarding which Client to operate Access Control in, and whether or not to use the default Client 000.

Question:

When we install the Access Control "System", the Access Control functionality by default is running in Client 000.

With SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, the Customer is restricted on what they can do in Client 000 as SAP ECS own and manage Client  000  

SAP_HANA_USER_MANAGEMENT_ABAP_Client_000.pdf

And so the question, when we implement/deploy SAP Access Control on S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, what is the "best practice recommendation from SAP" regarding the Client where Access Control is operating, should we operate Access Control on Client 000, or, should we operate Access Control in another Client, in a similar as is described for operating Process Control in a different Client 1505255 - Transfer client-specific Customizing for PC/RM 10.0 - SAP for Me

The worry is, if we operate Access Control in Client 000 on S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, since SAP ECS own Client 000, operating Access Control in 000, due to the Segregation of Duties between ECS and the Customer, operating Access Control could become difficult and complex.

What is the recommendation from SAP and other people's experience ?

Thanks and best regards,

Andy Silvey.

tagging:

@ElyasAhmed 

@T_Frenehard

entry.title: Re: The requested lookup key was not found in any active...
entry.content:
My program code has been modified as follows and it can be used normally now
Those who have questions can refer to it

using System;
using SAPbouiCOM.Framework;

namespace Attribute2
{
    class Program
    {
        public static SAPbouiCOM.Application SBO_Application;
        public static SAPbobsCOM.Company oCompany;
        public static string SelectedItemCode = "";

        [STAThread]
        static void Main(string[] args)
        {
            try
            {
                // ✅ 初始化 UI 应用程序（必须）Initialize the UI application (mandatory)
                Application app = args.Length < 1 ? new Application() : new Application(args[0]);

                // ✅ 获取 UI 应用对象 Obtain the UI application object
                SBO_Application = Application.SBO_Application;

                // ✅ 注册 AppEvent（关闭等）Register for AppEvent (close, etc.)
                SBO_Application.AppEvent += new SAPbouiCOM._IApplicationEvents_AppEventEventHandler(SBO_Application_AppEvent);

                // ✅ 获取 DI 公司对象 Obtain the object of DI Company
                oCompany = (SAPbobsCOM.Company)SBO_Application.Company.GetDICompany();

                if (!oCompany.Connected)
                {
                    SBO_Application.StatusBar.SetText("连接失败：无法获取 DI Company-Connection failure: DI Company cannot be obtained", SAPbouiCOM.BoMessageTime.bmt_Short, SAPbouiCOM.BoStatusBarMessageType.smt_Error);
                    return;
                }

                SBO_Application.StatusBar.SetText("连接成功！DI Company 已准备好-Connection successful! DI Company is ready", SAPbouiCOM.BoMessageTime.bmt_Short, SAPbouiCOM.BoStatusBarMessageType.smt_Success);

                // ✅ 添加菜单 Add menu
                Menu myMenu = new Menu();
                myMenu.AddMenuItems();

                // ✅ 注册菜单事件处理器 Register the menu event handler
                app.RegisterMenuEventHandler(myMenu.SBO_Application_MenuEvent);

                // ✅ 初始化自定义逻辑（事件监听、表单注册等） Initialize custom logic (event listening, form registration, etc.)
                setAttribute setA = new setAttribute(SBO_Application, oCompany);
                setA.Initialize();

                // ✅ 进入主循环 Enter the main cycle
                app.Run();
            }
            catch (Exception ex)
            {
                System.Windows.Forms.MessageBox.Show("启动失败：" + ex.Message + "\n" + ex.StackTrace);
            }
        }

        static void SBO_Application_AppEvent(SAPbouiCOM.BoAppEventTypes EventType)
        {
            if (EventType == SAPbouiCOM.BoAppEventTypes.aet_ShutDown)
            {
                System.Windows.Forms.Application.Exit();
            }
        }
    }
}

entry.title: Re: In SuccessFactors PMGM, I would like to hide the old...
entry.content:
Hello 

Hope you're doing well!

The forms displayed under the "Team Overview" tab are controlled by the "Start of Review" setting within the route map. If your older form templates have their own associated route maps, you can try disabling the "Start of Review" setting in those route maps. After making this change, please check if it resolves the issue.

Thanks,

Amaan
